name: Feuerzangenbowle
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the orange peel, lemon peel, and cloves in a tea filter bag and tie it shut with white yarn.
2. Hang the tea bag into a copper kettle and pour in the wine.
3. Heat the mixture close to boiling point but ensure it never actually boils.
4. Place the kettle in the center of your table so guests can watch the ceremony.
5. Use a Feuerzange (or a barbecue grid if unavailable) to safely hold the sugar hat on its side above the kettle.
6. Once ready, dim the lights.
7. Pour rum onto the sugar hat using a ladle and light it.
8. Keep the flames burning by adding more rum until all the sugar has melted and dripped into the wine.
9. Remove the Feuerzange and the tea bag with spices.
10. Serve in heat-proof glasses.
Variation: Some prefer to add sugar and/or a small amount of orange juice (preferably freshly pressed).
